html:
Exclusive to:
Mushroom Lights are Don't Starve Together exclusive objects, introduced in A New Reign. There are two types of Mushroom Lights, Mushlight and Glowcap. Their respective Blueprints can be found by killing a Toadstool or from opening a Loot Stash after killing a Klaus.

Both Mushlight and Glowcap have 4 slots. In these, players can place Light Bulbs, Glow Berries, Lesser Glow Berries, Mushroom Spores, Festive Lights, Crustashines or Enlightened Shards for the lights to function. The lights also act like an Ice Box and slow down the spoilage of the items put inside by 75%, extending the spoilage time by four times. Light Bulbs that spoil inside a mushroom light will drop as rot on the ground nearby. If an Enlightened Shard is placed in the Mushroom Lights, all perishable items in the inventory will not spoil, making it a great infinite light source. Festive Lights can also be used for infinite source of light, as they will still glow even when they reach 0%.

Mushlight

The Mushlight is a light source that can accept all items listed above except for Mushroom Spores. The light emitted by a Mushlight will always be white, regardless of the colors of Festive Lights and Enlightened Shards placed inside.

The more items placed inside, the bigger the light radius will be. However, one Enlightened Shard is enough to make a Mushlight to emit max level lights.

Glowcap

The Glowcap is an advanced version of the Mushlight, having a more expensive recipe. Unlike the Mushlight, the Glowcap is able to accept Mushroom Spores. The players can customize its color by placing Mushroom Spores, Festive Lights and colored Enlightened Shards inside the Glowcap. Light Bulbs, Glow Berries, Crustashines and empty Enlightened Shards will produce white light.

By mixing multiple colors in a Glowcap, one can obtain different color tones besides of red, green, and blue:

  • Purple - 1 Red Spores, 3 Blue Spore
  • Pink - 3 Light Bulbs, 1 Red Spore
  • Yellow - 2 Red Spores, 2 Green Spores
  • Orange - 3 Red Spores, 1 Green Spore
  • White - 1 of each spore type, leaving 1 slot empty.
  • Cyan - 2 Blue Spores, 2 Green Spores

The more items placed inside, the bigger the light radius will be. However, one Enlightened Shard is enough to make a Glowcap to emit max level lights. An Enlightened Shard also stops all other items inside the Glowcap from perishing.

Skins

Glowcap Skins

  • The Woven - Elegant Lilycap Light skin was introduced during the official Year of the Carrat event in 2020.
  • The Woven - Elegant Mushlamp Post skin was introduced during the official Year of the Beefalo event in 2021.
  • The Loyal Eclectic Glowcap skin was awarded for watching 3 hours of Don't Starve Together on Twitch from November 23, 2022 to December 8, 2022.
  • The Loyal Jack-o'-lamppost was distributed to everyone for free along with the Hallowed Nights Returns 2024 update.
Woven - Elegant
Cawnival Collection
Cawnival Lamp
These mushrooms have a warm glow that reminds you of summer.
Woven - Elegant
Year of the Carrat Collection
Lilycap Light
If a suaver lamp was ever made out of a mushroom, the world has yet to see it.
See ingame
Woven - Elegant
Victorian Collection
Mushlamp Post
"Nothing creates an ambiance quite like a glowing fungus."
Loyal
Art Nouveau Collection
Eclectic Glowcap
Put this living work of art someplace where it will really shine!
Loyal
Hallowed Nights Collection
Jack-o'-lamppost
A pumpkin's grin, a flickering light, guiding ghosts through the Hallowed Night.

Mushlight Skins

  • The Woven - Elegant Festive Fungal Lamp skin was introduced during the official Winter's Feast event in 2019.
  • The Woven - Elegant Nouveau Mushlamp skin was introduced during the official Year of the Carrat event in 2020.
  • The Loyal Mosaic Mushlamp skin was awarded for watching 3 hours of Don't Starve Together on Twitch from January 19, 2023 to February 6, 2023.
  • The Loyal Jack-o'-lamptern was distributed to everyone for free along with the
Woven - Elegant
Merrymaker Collection
Festive Fungal Lamp
Merry and bright.
See ingame
Woven - Elegant
Year of the Carrat Collection
Nouveau Mushlamp
This mushlamp isn't any brighter a regular lamp [sic], but it'll make guests think you're extremely cultured.
See ingame
Loyal
Art Nouveau Collection
Mosaic Mushlamp
This mushlamp was made to dazzle.
Loyal
Hallowed Nights Collection
Jack-o'-lamptern
On Hallowed Nights, the wise ones stay, close to the glow that keeps dark at bay.
Woven - Distinguished
Cawnival Collection
Cawnival Egg Lamp
Eggsquisite illumination.

Trivia

  • The Mushlight and the Glowcap were introduced in the Warts and All update.
  • Some color combinations of the Glowcap will emit slightly smaller radius than white light, despite being 4 items placed.

plainText:
Exclusive to:
Mushroom Lights are Don't Starve Together exclusive objects, introduced in A New Reign. There are two types of Mushroom Lights, Mushlight and Glowcap. Their respective Blueprints can be found by killing a Toadstool or from opening a Loot Stash after killing a Klaus.
Both Mushlight and Glowcap have 4 slots. In these, players can place Light Bulbs, Glow Berries, Lesser Glow Berries, Mushroom Spores, Festive Lights, Crustashines or Enlightened Shards for the lights to function. The lights also act like an Ice Box and slow down the spoilage of the items put inside by 75%, extending the spoilage time by four times. Light Bulbs that spoil inside a mushroom light will drop as rot on the ground nearby. If an Enlightened Shard is placed in the Mushroom Lights, all perishable items in the inventory will not spoil, making it a great infinite light source. Festive Lights can also be used for infinite source of light, as they will still glow even when they reach 0%.
Mushlight[edit | edit source]
Mushlight
"The perfect addtion to any mush-Room."
Structure Info
Perk
Provides Light.
Consumes Light Bulbs, Glow Berries and Crustashines.
Contents receive 25% spoilage rate.
Can be Burned
Can be Hammered
Crafting
×1 ×1
Filters
Tier
Miscellaneous
"mushroom_light"
“Science makes it light up.”
–Wilson, when examining a Mushlight.
“Not as nice as a warm fire.”
–Willow, when examining a Mushlight.
“Is nice light. Soft.”
–Wolfgang, when examining a Mushlight.
“Abigail would have thought this was... pretty...”
–Wendy, when examining a Mushlight.
“THE ORGANIC IS SWITCHED ON”
–WX-78, when examining a Mushlight.
“Quite enthralling, no?”
–Wickerbottom, when examining a Mushlight.
“Soak in that all-natural light, Lucy.”
–Woodie, when examining a Mushlight.
“A rather pale light.”
–Maxwell, when examining a Mushlight.
“Tis a spötted spötlight!”
–Wigfrid, when examining a Mushlight.
“It's giving off a nice light.”
–Webber, when examining a Mushlight.
“I do like being able to see.”
–Warly, when examining a Mushlight.
“Glow friends! Hi!”
–Wormwood, when examining a Mushlight.
“How's it work without circuitry?”
–Winona, when examining a Mushlight.
“A spotted spotlight to chase away the dark.”
–Wortox, when examining a Mushlight.
“Glowy!”
–Wurt, when examining a Mushlight.
“My mom loves collecting odd lamps... I hope she's doing alright.”
–Walter, when examining a Mushlight.
“Light made without oil or matches never ceases to amaze me.”
–Wanda, when examining a Mushlight.
The Mushlight is a light source that can accept all items listed above except for Mushroom Spores. The light emitted by a Mushlight will always be white, regardless of the colors of Festive Lights and Enlightened Shards placed inside.
The more items placed inside, the bigger the light radius will be. However, one Enlightened Shard is enough to make a Mushlight to emit max level lights.
Glowcap[edit | edit source]
Glowcap
"Inspired by the lava lamp sciences."
Structure Info
Perk
Provides Light.
Consumes Light Bulbs, Glow Berries, Mushroom Spores and Crustashines.
Contents receive 25% spoilage rate.
Can be Burned
Can be Hammered
Crafting
×1 ×1 ×1
Filters
Tier
Miscellaneous
"mushroom_light2"
“Blue is obviously the most scientific color.”
–Wilson, when examining a Glowcap.
“Pfft, well that was a bright idea.”
–Willow, when examining a Glowcap.
“Tall mushy-room make very pretty light!”
–Wolfgang, when examining a Glowcap.
“Such a sickly fungal glow...”
–Wendy, when examining a Glowcap.
“IT HAS SHOWN ME THE LIGHT”
–WX-78, when examining a Glowcap.
“Red and blue spores produce lovely reading light when combined.”
–Wickerbottom, when examining a Glowcap.
“It illuminates with all the colors of the wind.”
–Woodie, when examining a Glowcap.
“A light to repel the encroaching shadow.”
–Maxwell, when examining a Glowcap.
“Behold! It blazes bright!”
–Wigfrid, when examining a Glowcap.
“We love all the colors!”
–Webber, when examining a Glowcap.
“I like a nice pale blue, personally.”
–Warly, when examining a Glowcap.
“Oh! Colors pretty”
–Wormwood, when examining a Glowcap.
“It lights up, even without filament.”
–Winona, when examining a Glowcap.
“I quite like it when it's red!”
–Wortox, when examining a Glowcap.
“Oooh, pretty color!”
–Wurt, when examining a Glowcap.
“Nature is full of surprises.”
–Walter, when examining a Glowcap.
“Alright, glowing mushrooms I can appreciate.”
–Wanda, when examining a Glowcap.
The Glowcap is an advanced version of the Mushlight, having a more expensive recipe. Unlike the Mushlight, the Glowcap is able to accept Mushroom Spores. The players can customize its color by placing Mushroom Spores, Festive Lights and colored Enlightened Shards inside the Glowcap. Light Bulbs, Glow Berries, Crustashines and empty Enlightened Shards will produce white light.
By mixing multiple colors in a Glowcap, one can obtain different color tones besides of red, green, and blue:
Purple - 1 Red Spores, 3 Blue Spore
Pink - 3 Light Bulbs, 1 Red Spore
Yellow - 2 Red Spores, 2 Green Spores
Orange - 3 Red Spores, 1 Green Spore
White - 1 of each spore type, leaving 1 slot empty.
Cyan - 2 Blue Spores, 2 Green Spores
The more items placed inside, the bigger the light radius will be. However, one Enlightened Shard is enough to make a Glowcap to emit max level lights. An Enlightened Shard also stops all other items inside the Glowcap from perishing.
Skins[edit | edit source]
Glowcap Skins[edit | edit source]

The Woven - Elegant Mushlamp Post skin was introduced during the official Year of the Beefalo event in 2021.
The Loyal Eclectic Glowcap skin was awarded for watching 3 hours of Don't Starve Together on Twitch from November 23, 2022 to December 8, 2022.
The Loyal Jack-o'-lamppost was distributed to everyone for free along with the Hallowed Nights Returns 2024 update.

title: Mushroom Lights
wikitext:
{{Exclusivity|Don't Starve Together}} '''Mushroom Lights''' are ''[[Don't Starve Together]]'' exclusive objects, introduced in ''[[A New Reign]]''. There are two types of Mushroom Lights, Mushlight and Glowcap. Their respective [[Blueprint]]s can be found by killing a [[Toadstool]] or from opening a [[Loot Stash]] after killing a [[Klaus]]. Both Mushlight and Glowcap have 4 slots. In these, players can place [[Light Bulb]]s, [[Glow Berry|Glow Berries]], [[Lesser Glow Berry|Lesser Glow Berries]], [[Mushroom Spore]]s, [[Festive Lights]], [[Crustashine]]s or [[Enlightened Shard]]s for the lights to function. The lights also act like an [[Ice Box]] and slow down the spoilage of the items put inside by 75%, extending the spoilage time by four times. Light Bulbs that spoil inside a mushroom light will drop as rot on the ground nearby. If an Enlightened Shard is placed in the Mushroom Lights, all perishable items in the inventory will not spoil, making it a great infinite light source. Consumes Light Bulbs, Glow Berries and Crustashines.

Unlike the Mushlight, the Glowcap is able to accept [[Mushroom Spore]]s. The players can customize its color by placing Mushroom Spores, [[Festive Lights]] and colored [[Enlightened Shard]]s inside the Glowcap. An Enlightened Shard also stops all other items inside the Glowcap from perishing. {{Clear}} == [[File:Skins Icon.png|32px|link=]] Skins == === Glowcap Skins === * The '''Woven - Elegant Lilycap Light''' skin was introduced during the official [[Year of the Carrat]] event in 2020. * The '''Woven - Elegant Mushlamp Post''' skin was introduced during the official [[Year of the Beefalo]] event in 2021. * The '''Loyal Eclectic Glowcap''' skin was awarded for watching 3 hours of ''Don't Starve Together'' on Twitch from November 23, 2022 to December 8, 2022. * The '''Loyal Jack-o'-lamppost''' was distributed to everyone for free along with the [[From Beyond#Hallowed Nights Returns 2024|Hallowed Nights Returns 2024]] update.

=== Mushlight Skins === * The '''Woven - Elegant Festive Fungal Lamp''' skin was introduced during the official [[Winter's Feast]] event in 2019. * The '''Woven - Elegant Nouveau Mushlamp''' skin was introduced during the official [[Year of the Carrat]] event in 2020. * The '''Loyal Mosaic Mushlamp''' skin was awarded for watching 3 hours of ''Don't Starve Together'' on Twitch from January 19, 2023 to February 6, 2023. * The '''Loyal Jack-o'-lamptern''' was distributed to everyone for free along with the
{{SkinInfo |image = Festive Fungal Lamp Icon.png |rarity = Woven - Elegant |collection = Merrymaker Collection |name = Festive Fungal Lamp |description = Merry and bright. |ingame = Festive Fungal Lamp.png }} {{SkinInfo |image = Nouveau Mushlamp Icon.png |rarity = Woven - Elegant |collection = Year of the Carrat Collection |name = Nouveau Mushlamp |description = This mushlamp isn't any brighter a regular lamp [sic], but it'll make guests think you're extremely cultured. |ingame = Nouveau Mushlamp.png }} {{SkinInfo |image = Mosaic Mushlamp Icon.png |rarity = Loyal |collection = Art Nouveau Collection |name = Mosaic Mushlamp |description = This mushlamp was made to dazzle. }} {{SkinInfo |image = Jack-o'-lamptern Icon.png |rarity = Loyal |collection = Hallowed Nights Collection |name = Jack-o'-lamptern |description = On Hallowed Nights, the wise ones stay, close to the glow that keeps dark at bay. }} {{SkinInfo |image = Cawnival Egg Lamp Icon.png |rarity = Woven - Distinguished |collection = Cawnival Collection |name = Cawnival Egg Lamp |description = Eggsquisite illumination. }}
